description: Batch regenerates image_prompt for specified scenes using the visual_director prompt and each scene's narration_text. Returns immediately; updated prompts written via Supabase.

async_steps.name: Regenerate image prompts
actions: For each scene in the batch: call Claude with visual_director prompt + scene narration_text + visual_type + emotional_beat + chapter context
Parse response to extract new image_prompt for each scene
Batch scenes into groups of 10-20 per Claude call for efficiency (pass multiple scenes in one prompt)

async_steps.name: Update scenes
actions: For each scene: PATCH /rest/v1/scenes?id=eq.{scene_id} SET image_prompt=new_prompt
Also update the scene's image_prompt in topics.script_json.scenes array: read-modify-write on topics.script_json
POST /rest/v1/production_log INSERT { stage: 'prompt_regeneration', action: 'completed', details: { scene_count: N, scene_ids } }

error_handling.on_claude_api_error: Log error to production_log. Partial success is OK — scenes that succeeded keep their new prompts, failed scenes retain original prompts.
error_handling.on_supabase_write_error: Retry individual scene PATCH once. Log failures.
error_handling.on_empty_scene_ids: Return { success: false, error: 'No scene IDs provided' } synchronously (do not start async).

realtime_events.scenes.image_prompt: Updated per scene as prompts are regenerated

notes: This is a batch operation — the dashboard collects edited scenes and sends all IDs at once.
The 'Regenerate Edited Prompts' toolbar button in Script Review triggers this endpoint.
Image prompts are regenerated using the visual_director prompt, not the original script generation prompts.
Both the scenes table rows AND the topics.script_json are updated to stay in sync.
Partial success is acceptable — the dashboard can show which scenes were updated.
